ol.li: must be capable of being deactivated when the building or part of a building served by that system is not occupied; and

must not use a single heating coil or a single cooling coil in an air-handling unit to serve more than one air-conditioning zone where one zone will require heating at the same time another requires cooling; and

when serving more than one air-conditioning zone or area with different heating or cooling needs, must—
thermostatically control the temperature of each zone or area; and
not control the temperature by mixing actively heated air and actively cooled air; and
serve diverse loads through modulating airflow to the maximum extent practicable; and
limit zone-level reheating of air cooled by equipment upstream of the zone to not more than 7.5°C at any intended airflow rate; and
limit zone-level re-cooling of air heated by equipment upstream of the zone to not more than 7.5°C at any intended airflow rate; and

which provides the required mechanical ventilation, other than in climate zone 1 or where dehumidification control is needed, must have an outdoor air economy cycle if the total air flow rate of any airside component of an air-conditioning system is greater than or equal to the flow rates in Table J6D3; and
which contains more than one water heater, chiller or coil, must be capable of stopping the flow of water to those not operating; and
when serving a sole-occupancy unit in a Class 3 building, must not operate when any external door of the sole-occupancy unit is open for more than one minute; and
must have the ability to use direct signals from the control components responsible for the delivery of comfort conditions in the building to regulate the operation of central plant; and
must have a control dead band of not less than 2°C, except where a smaller range is required for specialised applications; and

must be provided with balancing dampers and balancing valves, as required to meet the needs of the system at its maximum operating condition, that ensure the maximum design air or fluid flow is achieved but not exceeded by more than 15% above design at each—
component; or
group of components operating under a common control in a system containing multiple components; and

must ensure that each independently operating space of more than 1000 m2 and every separate floor of the building has provision to terminate airflow independently of the remainder of the system sufficient to allow for different operating times; and
where the design maximum heated water temperature is greater than 45°C, must be capable of automatic variable temperature operation of heated water circuits; and
when deactivated, must close any motorised outdoor air or return air damper that is not otherwise being actively controlled; and
must be capable of automatic variable temperature operation of chilled water circuits; and

where fitted with a cooling tower, must automatically vary the condenser water set point temperature across the full range of condenser water inlet temperatures permitted by the equipment being served.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: When two or more air-conditioning systems serve the same space they must use control sequences that prevent the systems from operating in opposing heating and cooling modes.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: A time switch must be provided to control—

ol.li: an air-conditioning system of more than 2 kWr; and
a heater of more than 1 kWheating used for air-conditioning.
be capable of switching electric power on and off at variable pre-programmed times and on variable pre-programmed days; and
have the ability to program public holidays at least 1 year ahead; and

be capable of being overridden by—
a demand-operated control; or
a manual switch; and
allow the resumption of normal operation no longer than 2 hours after any override is activated.

subclause.title: SubClause
subclause.p: The requirements of (3) and (4) do not apply to—

ol.li:
an air-conditioning system that serves—
only one sole-occupancy unit in a Class 3 or 9c building; or
only a Class 4 part of a building; or
a conditioned space where air-conditioning is needed for 24 hour continuous use.

subclause.title: NSW REPLACE SubClause
subclause.p: The requirements of (3) and (4) do not apply to—

ol.li: an air-conditioning system that serves only one sole-occupancy unit in a Class 3 or 9c building; or
a conditioned space where air-conditioning is needed for 24 hour continuous use.

callout.p: In J6D3(1)(c)(iii) airflow should not be modulated—

ol.li: below the level needed for required ventilation; or
beyond a level of pressurisation appropriate to the use of the space, in accordance with AS 1668.2; or
so that it interferes with the operation of the air-conditioning unit.
